provenance.source_corpus: Changes in microbial community structure and function following Sphagnum peatland restoration

source_spans.text: Changes in microbial community structure and function following Sphagnum peatland restoration
recovery of particular plant groups, namely mosses and shrubs in restored peatlands might be the driver of changes occurring in the structure of the microbial communities in restored peatlands
Complete Sphagnum cover is associated with greater decomposition of carbon compounds
The decomposition potential increased with increasing Sphagnum thickness, but also with pH
high bulk density are related to lower decomposition potential
The presence of ericaceous shrubs can also reduce the microbial activity through the inhibitory activity of phenolic allelochemicals found in ericaceous humus
combination of fresh organic matter (low bulk density + presence of mosses) and the absence of ericaceous shrubs create conditions favourable for the development of an active microbial biomass
Complete Sphagnum cover is associated with greater decomposition of carbon compounds
The decomposition potential increased with increasing Sphagnum thickness, but also with pH
The decomposition potential increased with increasing Sphagnum thickness, but also with pH
high bulk density are related to lower decomposition potential
high proportion of bacteria and high bulk density are related to lower decomposition potential
The presence of ericaceous shrubs can also reduce the microbial activity through the inhibitory activity of phenolic allelochemicals found in ericaceous humus
combination of fresh organic matter (low bulk density + presence of mosses) and the absence of ericaceous shrubs create conditions favourable for the development of an active microbial biomass
The presence of ericaceous shrubs can also reduce the microbial activity through the inhibitory activity of phenolic allelochemicals found in ericaceous humus

edges.original_sentence: Overall, this study demonstrates that the recovery of particular plant groups, namely mosses and shrubs in restored peatlands might be the driver of changes occurring in the structure of the microbial communities in restored peatlands.
